# Header Skill

> A skill for agentic coding tools that **audits and optimizes** the AI coding agent's own setup — `CLAUDE.md`, model, dependencies, settings — and enriches recommendations with the latest agentic-coding briefing from [Header](https://joinheader.com). Built for [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) but works in any agent with shell access (Cursor, Aider, OpenAI Codex CLI, Goose). Public briefings work with no authentication; an API key unlocks custom topics tuned to your repo.

Header is the optimization layer for AI coding agents. Every invocation of this skill runs a local audit of the agent harness (prompt-config debt + supply-chain posture) and joins those findings with the latest agentic-coding briefing for the resolved topic. Briefings are **input** to the audit, not its primary output: developments in the briefing `summary` about your stack become recommendations alongside the local scans. Core Header concepts: **Topics** group sources and goals, **Goals** define what to learn, **Source Groups** are collections of content feeds, and **Briefings** are the synthesized digests produced by analyzing recent content against a goal.

This skill is a small, declarative folder: `SKILL.md` drives the agent to run `bin/header-audit`, call Header's public API via `curl`, read the current workspace, and propose actionable changes; `bin/header-config` persists configuration under `~/.header/`; `install.sh` installs the folder into an agent's skills directory. No runtime, no build step, no dependencies beyond `bash` and `curl`. The audit is always local — no code or file contents leave the machine; generic enrichment egresses nothing project-derived, and choosing **custom** (codebase-tuned) enrichment sends only a one-line stack summary to build the briefing (an API key; the skill can set up a free anonymous trial account for it).

## Skill files

- [header/SKILL.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/SKILL.md): The skill definition. Contains the run-first preamble, the default audit + briefing-enrichment flow (Steps 0-4, with output modifiers `summary` / `sources`), the **session wrap-up & compound** flow (`/header wrapup` / `/header compound` — native capture of session learnings/pitfalls into committed `.claude/memory/`, draft-then-ask, team-vs-personal disposition), the first-run onboarding (welcome, language preference), the post-audit custom-topic offer + signup walkthrough + chained per-repo offers (bind, schedule, team config), telemetry consent, public topic browsing, the custom-briefing (API key) workflow, and the full response-field reference.
- [header/bin/header-audit](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-audit): Local, read-only scan that drives the default flow — `harness` (prompt-config debt + Bash-tool permission posture + the always-loaded context tax — skills + command/subagent registry frontmatter, split repo→📦 project / user→💻 local; static so it reports on a fresh clone), `deps` (dependency / supply-chain posture), `waste` (transcript-mined usage accounting: MCP servers / skills configured but never used, per-tool error rates, compaction pressure — repo-scoped like `cost`, `--all-projects` opt-in), `rails` (determinism guardrails present|absent — pre-commit gate / test ratchet / compounding memory), `retro` (the COACH scan: behavioral mining of your own sessions — edit-thrash, gotcha volume, git-workflow tells, and the derived `RETRO-CAP` capability nudges that map behavior→practice: worktree / guardrail / compound), `grade` (TWO composite, deterministic SETUP GRADES over the five scorecard axes, composited from harness+deps+rails and partitioned by scope: **📦 project** — the repo's checked-in config, reproducible across machines, the headline `GRADE`/`GRADE-AXIS` rows shown on howsmyaicoding.com; and **💻 local harness** — your machine (`~/.claude`, the model you run, tool versions), the `GRADE-LOCAL`/`GRADE-AXIS-LOCAL` rows, never folded into the project grade. Static-config only so the project grade is stable with or without session history; computed in the bin, never model-assigned), `rail <name>` (print the stack-adapted scaffold to install a guardrail), `patterns` (list the built-in cargo-cult prompt-debt patterns), `gate npm|pip` (print the recommended supply-chain snippet). Every recommendation-capable line carries a final `key=<canonical-key>` field — the pre-minted, deterministic recommendation-ledger key (the agent uses it verbatim; model-minted keys fragmented cross-run dedup). Plain bash; nothing leaves the machine.
- [header/bin/header-cost](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-cost): BETA. Local "billing meter" — `report` (measured spend by model from usage JSONL or raw Claude Code transcripts), `cost`, `prices`, `refresh`. Cache writes priced by real 5m/1h duration; legacy Opus priced apart from current Opus. No projections (`savings` only points at the experiment loop). Plain bash.
- [header/bin/header-experiment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-experiment): BETA. Local A/B (and A/A) experimentation engine — closes the audit → experiment → applied-change loop, with cost-vs-magnitude gating so the agent doesn't burn tokens proving tiny effects. Subcommands: `new` (audit-aware scaffolder: `--kind prompt-debt-deletion` strips lines into arm B AND prints a magnitude estimate that surfaces `[Apply with review]` for <5% changes; `--kind clause-add` for INSERTION experiments — mandatory-skill / delegation / fast-mode toggles; `--kind model-swap`; or generic `--arm`; `--task` is repeatable for multi-task experiments), `define` (low-level placeholder), `validate`, `run` (worktree-isolated execution via Claude Code headless or a stub adapter, with `--aa` for noise-floor and `--jobs N` wave-parallel (task,rep) blocks — arms stay sequential per pair so pairing holds; cost gate speaks BOTH billing modes — API $ + subscription headroom — and lists cheap-experiment levers; `worktree_include:` spec field symlinks venv/.env/etc into the worktree to fix the editable-install false-success class), `analyze` (paired-by-task bootstrap CI + success non-inferiority; soft N=1 / N=2-4 / N≥5 power tiers; A/A with N=1×K reps switches to replicate-level bootstrap for real bias detection; agent-error rows are excluded from cost means but COUNT on the success axis with the verifier's verdict, so a flaky arm can't look non-inferior by crashing), `report` (scorecard with the §6.5 decision rule + conservative savings rate), `merge` (apply arm B's overrides after a B-wins verdict; shows the diff, suggests a `git commit` with the `Header-Audit-Finding:` trailer), `push` (manual sync; also runs AUTOMATICALLY on every lifecycle edit — new/define/validate/run/analyze/merge — when an API key is present, gated by config `experiment_sync` auto|off, no per-edit prompt; no key → a once-per-experiment recommendation) — sends an experiment's lineage + status + verdict to the user's own account so it shows in the web UI: experiment/hypothesis/topic+goal+briefing/repo/machine/result, with a last-known status (defined→run→analyzed→merged); metadata only, prompt bodies/override contents/logs never leave (prompts identified by sha256 + a descriptive title: authored→derived-from-first-heading→id); `--dry-run` renders the payload offline, `--all` batches; the first slice of the org dashboard, distinct from the anonymized cross-customer aggregate below; backend `POST /api/v2/experiments` is live), `aggregate` (ANONYMIZED effect-size submit to the cross-customer proven-changes library — kind, curated category, ecosystem, arm engines, result CIs; no identity/repo/prompts/free text, POST carries no API key; opt-in via `aggregate_submit` config (personal-only, default off) or per-call y/N / `--yes`; `--dry-run` previews; the library feeds back as 6-field proven `patterns.tsv` rows that `header-audit` re-emits as `PROVEN` lines). Pre-spend honesty: a degenerate-CI warning + the measured A/A noise floor surfaced at the gate, and `--yes` discloses cost/power (skips the prompt, not the disclosure). Discrimination guards on BOTH axes: validate/run warn (escalating on `MUST`/`NEVER`/`ALWAYS` mandates; hand-rolled specs caught by diffing arm overrides vs the repo) when a prompt-debt deletion can't be measured on the success axis, and report warns on the cost axis (arm A not measurably costlier → the adapter may not have performed the mandate); mandate deletions surface the guardrail-value recommendation (measure one execution's cost, don't A/B tail-risk). Stateful experiments: `setup:` / `teardown:` / `setup_scope:` (experiment|run) provision an isolated DB/branch, inject its connection info into the adapter+verifier, and tear it down via a guaranteed EXIT/INT/TERM trap. Implements §§8–10 + §12 + §6.8 (magnitude-vs-cost rule) of the experiments design. Does NOT yet mine tasks from git history (§11), do σ-based power analysis, or aggregate across customers. Plain bash.
- [header/bin/header-config](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-config): Reads/writes the persisted config at `~/.header/config` (get/set/list/defaults), plus team-config commands (`team-init`, `team-set`, `team-get`, `team-show`, `team-path`) for committed `<repo>/.header/config`. Plain bash.
- [header/bin/header-repo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-repo): Remembers which custom topic a repository uses (bind/get/clear/list/seen) and per-repo onboarding flags (topic-offered, schedule-offered, team-config-offered), so the right offers fire once per repo. Local registry at `~/.header/repos.jsonl`, keyed by git remote (path fallback). Plain bash.
- [header/bin/header-ledger](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-ledger): Local recommendation ledger — record/status/get/list `applied`, `dismissed`, `snoozed`, `wanted` actions per key (`get` returns a key's full JSON record — title/briefing/topic/source — so an experiment sync can recover a finding's provenance). Records are keyed by the normalized git remote (toplevel-path fallback, header-repo's convention); legacy basename-keyed records still match on reads, and a directory passed via `--repo` resolves to that repo's identity while any other value stays literal. Drives diff-aware briefings and the goal auto-tuner. Plain bash; nothing leaves the machine.
- [header/bin/header-telemetry](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-telemetry): Local opt-in usage logger — three tiers (off / anonymous / full); sends usage metadata only (path, outcome, skill version, OS, counts). Never sends code, file paths, repo / branch names, or briefing content. Plain bash.
- [header/bin/header-update-check](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/bin/header-update-check): Polls `/api/v2/skill/version` (cached + snoozed) and emits one of `UP_TO_DATE`, `UPDATE_AVAILABLE old new`, or `UPDATE_REQUIRED old min`. The preamble surfaces it; the SKILL.md "Staying up to date" flow handles it.
- [header/VERSION](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/header/VERSION): The skill version (mirrors the `SKILL.md` frontmatter `version:`; a version-parity test guards drift).
- [install.sh](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/install.sh): One-command installer — copies the `header/` folder into the agent's skills directory (`~/.claude/skills/header`, plus `$CODEX_HOME/skills/header` ≈ `~/.codex/skills/header` when `codex` is on `PATH`), makes `bin/` executable, atomic swap with rollback. As of 0.10.0 also removes any legacy `header-briefing/` at the same skills root. The installable skill is the `header/` subfolder — the repo root has no `SKILL.md`. Codex loads skills at startup, so restart Codex after install; the preamble self-heals stripped exec bits (`HEADER_SELFHEAL:`) and flags an unwritable `~/.header` sandbox (`HEADER_STATE: readonly`).
- [README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/README.md): Installation, usage, and configuration.
- [ROADMAP.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/ROADMAP.md): Planned (`header-experiment` engine) and deferred (`/plugin` marketplace) work.
- [docs/experiments-design.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/docs/experiments-design.md): Full design spec for the experiment engine (Header's optimization-by-experimentation thesis, A/A noise floor, statistics, hybrid runner architecture, verifier mining from git history, `header-experiment` CLI spec).
- [LICENSE](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Header-inc/Header-skill/main/LICENSE): MIT.

## Header API endpoints used by this skill

- `GET https://joinheader.com/api/v2/topics/public/{topic_id}`: Public topic metadata, including `latest_briefing.id`. Default topic is the "Self Improving Agent" briefing at `1991163f-be9c-4df2-a33c-046a4d1357e1`.
- `GET https://joinheader.com/api/v2/public/briefings/{briefing_id}`: Full briefing (summary, key_developments, source_articles). No auth required. JSON only — `Accept: text/markdown` is not honored on this endpoint.
- `GET https://joinheader.com/api/v2/topics/public/catalog`: Browse all public topics.
- `POST https://joinheader.com/api/v2/topics/`: Create a custom topic (requires `Authorization: Bearer $HEADER_API_KEY`). Default source group `64981a34-3b8b-4064-a391-22f4534c229b` covers AI agent frameworks, MCP, and coding tools.
- `POST https://joinheader.com/api/v2/goals/{goal_id}/briefings`: Trigger a new briefing for an existing goal (requires API key). Returns `201` with `estimated_duration_seconds` ETA.
- `GET https://joinheader.com/api/v2/briefings/{briefing_id}`: Check status of a private briefing (`IN_PROGRESS`, `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`). Honors `Accept: text/markdown` for a pre-rendered markdown response.
- `PUT https://joinheader.com/api/v2/goals/{goal_id}`: Update goal description, keywords, `memory_enabled` (kicks off async Forge provisioning; poll `memory_state` until `enabled`/`error`), or scheduling (`schedule_enabled`, `schedule_frequency_days` for 3/7/14/30-day auto-regeneration). `GET` returns `last_briefing_at` and `next_scheduled_generation`.
- `GET https://joinheader.com/api/v2/topics/dashboard?since=<iso8601>`: Agent-shaped dashboard — returns topics with status changes since the timestamp, plus a server-computed `next_action` enum (`briefing_ready` / `briefing_failed` / `briefing_in_progress` / `nothing`) for cron / scheduled runs.

## Header product docs

- [Header docs home](https://joinheader.com/docs): Overview of the service, core concepts, and API.
- [Header dashboard](https://joinheader.com): Sign up, manage topics, and create API keys (`Settings > API Keys`, scope `read + write`).

## Optional

- [Claude Code skills documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/skills): How skills are loaded and invoked.
- [llms.txt specification](https://llmstxt.org): The format this file follows.
